description: Public sentiment regarding the collapse of Enron in late 2001 following revelations of massive systemic accounting fraud. Pro-regulation sentiment surged as investors demanded stricter oversight of energy trading and auditing practices.

key_events.description: Enron announces massive third-quarter loss and SEC investigation begins.
Sherron Watkins sends warning letter to Kenneth Lay about accounting irregularities.
Enron files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy; national outrage peaks.
Congressional hearings begin into Arthur Andersen's role in the fraud.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating trust-collapse trajectories and the speed at which systemic corporate failure translates into public demand for regulatory intervention.
